questions.question_text: Show that the points A(1, – 2, – 8), B (5, 0, – 2) and C (11, 3, 7) are collinear, and find the ratio in which B divides AC.
questions.question_html: Show that the points A(1, – 2, – 8), B (5, 0, – 2) and C (11, 3, 7) are collinear, and find the ratio in which B divides AC.
questions.explanation: {tex}\overrightarrow {AB} = 4\widehat i + 2\widehat j + 6\widehat k = 2\left( {2\widehat i + \widehat j + 3\widehat k} \right){/tex} {tex} \overrightarrow {BC} = 6\widehat i + 3\widehat j + 9\widehat k = 3(2\widehat i + \widehat j + 3\widehat k){/tex} {tex} \therefore \overrightarrow {AB} = 2 \times \frac{{\overrightarrow {BC} }}{3} {/tex} Therefore , AB and BC are parallel, but point B is common , so points , A,B,C are collinear. As {tex}\frac{{\overrightarrow {AB} }}{{\overrightarrow {BC} }} = \frac{2}{3}{/tex} , thus , point B divides AC in the ratio 2 : 3.

questions.question_text: Find the position vector of a point R which divides the line joining two points P and Q whose position vectors are {tex}\left( {2\vec a + \vec b} \right){/tex} and {tex}\left( {\vec a - 3\vec b} \right){/tex} externally in the ratio 1 : 2. Also, show that P is the mid point of the line segment RQ.
questions.question_html: Find the position vector of a point R which divides the line joining two points P and Q whose position vectors are {tex}\left( {2\vec a + \vec b} \right){/tex}and{tex}\left( {\vec a - 3\vec b} \right){/tex} externally in the ratio 1 : 2. Also, show that P is the mid point of the line segment RQ.
questions.explanation: Let position vector of point R be {tex}\overrightarrow r {/tex} . As point R divides externally the line segment PQ in the ratio 1:2 .therefore , {tex} \overrightarrow r = \frac{{1\left( {\overrightarrow a - 3\overrightarrow b } \right) - 2\left( {2\overrightarrow a + \overrightarrow b } \right)}}{{1 - 2}} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{\left( { - 3\overrightarrow a - 5\overrightarrow b } \right)}}{{ - 1}}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow r = 3\overrightarrow a + 5\overrightarrow b \\ {/tex} Also , mid point of the line segment RQ is : {tex}= \frac{{3\overrightarrow a + 5\overrightarrow b + \overrightarrow a - 3\overrightarrow b }}{2} = \frac{{4\overrightarrow a + 2\overrightarrow b }}{2} = 2\overrightarrow a + \overrightarrow b{/tex} ,w hich is the position vector of point P.Therefore , P is the mid point of line segment RQ.

questions.explanation: Let {tex}\overrightarrow{d}=\lambda (\overrightarrow{a}\times \overrightarrow{b}),{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec d = \lambda \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&{\hat j}&{\hat k} \\ 1&4&2 \\ 3&{ - 2}&7 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}=\lambda(32\hat i -\hat j-14 \hat k){/tex} Also, {tex}\vec c.\vec d{/tex} = 15 {tex}\Rightarrow (2\hat i -\hat j+ 4\hat k).\lambda(32 \hat i -\hat j-14 \hat k)=15{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 9\lambda=15\Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{5}{3}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec d=\frac{5}{3}(32 \hat i -\hat j-14 \hat k){/tex} {tex}=\frac{160}{3}\hat i-\frac{5}{3}\hat j -\frac{70}{3}\hat k{/tex}

questions.question_html: The scalar product of the vector {tex}\hat i + \hat j + \hat k{/tex} with a unit vector along the sum of vectors {tex}2\hat i + 4\hat j - 5\hat k\;{/tex} and {tex}\lambda \hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} is equal to one. Find the value of{tex}\;\lambda {/tex}.
questions.explanation: Let {tex}\overrightarrow{a}=\widehat{i}+\widehat{j}+\widehat{k},\overrightarrow{b}=2\widehat{i}+4\widehat{j}-5\widehat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{c}=\lambda \widehat{i}+2\widehat{j}+3\widehat{k},{/tex} {tex}\vec b +\vec c =(\lambda+2)\hat i +6\hat j-2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec b+\vec c|=\sqrt{(\lambda+2)^2+40}{/tex} Therefore, a unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{b}+\overrightarrow{c}{/tex} is given by: {tex}\frac{\vec b +\vec c}{|\vec b+\vec c|}=\frac{(\lambda+2)\hat i +6 \hat j-2\hat k}{\sqrt{(\lambda+2)^2+40}}=1{/tex} Also, scalar product of {tex}\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} with above unit vector is 1. {tex}\therefore(\hat i+ \hat j+\hat k).\frac{(\lambda+2)\hat i +6 \hat j-2\hat k}{\sqrt{(\lambda+2)^2+40}}=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (\lambda+6)^2=(\lambda+2)^2+40\Rightarrow \lambda=1{/tex}

Which is the required solution.

questions.question_html: Find a unit vector perpendicular to each of the vector {tex}\vec a + \vec b\;{/tex} and {tex}\vec a - \vec b{/tex}, where {tex}\vec a = 3\hat i + 2\hat j + 2\hat k\;{/tex} and {tex}\;\vec b = \hat i + 2\hat j - 2\hat k{/tex}.
questions.explanation: It is given that: {tex}\vec a = 3\hat i+2 \hat j+2 \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b=\hat i +2 \hat j-2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a +\vec b=4\hat i+4\hat j{/tex} and {tex}\vec a -\vec b=2\hat i +4 \hat k{/tex} {tex}\therefore (\vec a + \vec b) \times (\vec a - \vec b) = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&{\hat j}&{\hat k} \\ 4&4&0 \\ 2&0&4 \end{array}} \right| = 16\hat i - 16\hat j - 8\hat k{/tex} {tex}\therefore| (\vec a + \vec b) \times (\vec a - \vec b) |= \sqrt{576}=24{/tex} Therefore, the unit vector perpendicular to both the vectors {tex}(\overrightarrow{a}+\overrightarrow{b}){/tex} and {tex}(\overrightarrow{a}-\overrightarrow{b}){/tex} is given by: {tex}=\pm \frac{(16\widehat{i}-16\widehat{j}-8\widehat{k})}{24}=\pm \frac{1}{3}(2\widehat{i}-2\widehat{j}-\widehat{k}) .{/tex}

questions.question_text: If a unit vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} makes angles {tex} \frac{\pi }{3}\;with\;{\hat i }\; \frac{{\pi }}{{4}}\;with\; {\hat j}\; and\; an\; acute \;angle\; \theta\; with\; {\hat k}{/tex} , then find {tex}\theta {/tex}
questions.question_html: If a unit vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} makes angles {tex} \frac{\pi }{3}\;with\;{\hat i }\; \frac{{\pi }}{{4}}\;with\; {\hat j}\; and\; an\; acute \;angle\; \theta\; with\; {\hat k}{/tex} , then find {tex}\theta {/tex}
questions.explanation: Let {tex}\overrightarrow{a}={{a}_{1}}\widehat{i}+{{a}_{2}}\widehat{j}+{{a}_{3}}\widehat{k},{/tex} It is given that {tex}\left| \overrightarrow{a} \right|=1{/tex} , then, {tex}\Rightarrow a^2_1+a^2_2+a^2_3=1{/tex} ....(1) {tex}\therefore \vec a.\hat i =(a_1\hat i+a_2\hat j+a_3\hat k).\hat i{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec a||\hat i|cos\frac{\pi}{3}=a_1 \Rightarrow a_1=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a.\hat j =(a_1\hat i+a_2\hat j+a_3\hat k).\hat j{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec a||\hat j|cos\frac{\pi}{4}=a_2 \Rightarrow a_2=\frac{1}{\sqrt2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a.\hat k =(a_1\hat i+a_2\hat j+a_3\hat k).\hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec a||\hat k|cos{\theta}=a_3 \Rightarrow a_3=\cos\theta{/tex} Putting these values in (1), we get: {tex}\frac{1}{4} + \frac{1}{2} + {\cos ^2}\theta = 1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3}{4} = 1 - {\cos ^2}\theta \Rightarrow {\sin ^2}\theta = \frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow \sin \theta = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} \Rightarrow \theta = {60^ \circ }{/tex}

questions.explanation: Let, {tex}\overrightarrow a = {a_1}\hat i + {a_2}\hat j + {a_3}\hat k{/tex} , then, {tex}\Rightarrow a^2_1+a^2_2+a_3^2=1{/tex} .....(1) {tex}\therefore \vec a.\hat i = (a_1\hat i +a_2 \hat j+a_3\hat k).\hat i\Rightarrow |\vec a||\hat i|cos\frac{\pi}{3}=a_1\Rightarrow a_1=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a.\hat j= (a_1\hat i +a_2 \hat j+a_3\hat k).\hat j\Rightarrow |\vec a||\hat j|cos\frac{\pi}{4}=a_2\Rightarrow a_2=\frac{1}{\sqrt2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a.\hat k= (a_1\hat i +a_2 \hat j+a_3\hat k).\hat k\Rightarrow |\vec a||\hat k|cos\frac{\pi}{4}=a_3\Rightarrow a_3=\cos\theta{/tex} Putting these values in (1) , we get : {tex}\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{2}+cos^2\theta=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3}{4}=1-cos^2\theta \Rightarrow sin^2\theta=\frac{3}{4}\Rightarrow sin\theta =\frac{\sqrt3}{2}\Rightarrow \theta =60^o{/tex} {tex}\therefore a_3=cos60^o=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec a=\frac{1}{2}\hat i+\frac{1}{\sqrt2}\hat j+\frac{1}{2}\hat k{/tex}

questions.question_text: Find {tex}\lambda \;and\;\mu \;if\;\left( {2\hat i + 6\hat j + 27\hat k} \right) \times \left( {\hat i + \lambda \hat j + \mu \hat k} \right) = \vec 0{/tex}
questions.question_html: Find {tex}\lambda \;and\;\mu \;if\;\left( {2\hat i + 6\hat j + 27\hat k} \right) \times \left( {\hat i + \lambda \hat j + \mu \hat k} \right) = \vec 0{/tex}
questions.explanation: It is given that: {tex}\left( {2\hat i + 6\hat j + 27\hat k} \right)X\left( {\hat i + \lambda \hat j + \mu \hat k} \right) = \overrightarrow 0 {/tex} {tex}\left| {\matrix{ {\hat i} & {\hat j} & {\hat k} \cr 2 & 6 & {27} \cr 1 & \lambda & \mu \cr } } \right| = \hat i\left( {6\mu - 27\lambda } \right) - \hat j\left( {2\mu - 27} \right) + \hat k\left( {2\lambda - 6} \right) = \overrightarrow 0 {/tex} , equating the coefficients of {tex}\hat i,\hat j,\hat k{/tex} on both sides,we get {tex}\left( {6\mu - 27\lambda } \right) = 0,\left( {2\mu - 27} \right) = 0,\left( {2\lambda - 6} \right) = 0.{/tex} solving,we get {tex}{\lambda = 3,\mu = {{27} \over 2}}{/tex}

questions.explanation: It is given that {tex}\overrightarrow{a}\times \overrightarrow{b}{/tex} is a unit vector, then: {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec a\times \vec b|=1\Rightarrow |\vec a||\vec b|sin\theta =1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 3.\frac{\sqrt2}{3}sin\theta=1\Rightarrow sin\theta =\frac{1}{\sqrt2}\Rightarrow \theta =\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_text: Find the area of the triangle with vertices A(1, 1, 2), B(2, 3, 5) and C(1, 5, 5).
questions.question_html: Find the area of the triangle with vertices A(1, 1, 2), B(2, 3, 5) and C(1, 5, 5).
questions.explanation: Given position vector of A , {tex}\overrightarrow {OA} = \hat i + \hat j + 2\hat k{/tex} position vector of B, {tex}\overrightarrow {OB} = 2\hat i + 3\hat j + 5\hat k{/tex} and that of C, {tex}\overrightarrow {OC} = \hat i + 5\hat j + 5\hat k{/tex} therefore, {tex}\overrightarrow {AB} = \overrightarrow {OB} - \overrightarrow {OA} = \left( {2\hat i + 3\hat j + 5\hat k} \right) - \left( {\hat i + \hat j + 2\hat k} \right) = \hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} (by triangle law of vector addition) thus we may write {tex}\vec{AB}=\hat i +2\hat j+3\hat k{/tex} , {tex}\vec{AC}=4\hat j+3\hat k,{/tex} {tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{AB}\times \overrightarrow{AC}=\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&{\hat j}&{\hat k} \\ 1&2&3 \\ 0&4&3 \end{array}} \right|=-6\hat i-3\hat j+4\hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\overrightarrow{AB}\times\vec{AC}|=\sqrt{61}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\frac{1}{2} |\overrightarrow{AB}\times\vec{AC}|=\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{61}{/tex} Therefore, the area of triangle ABC is = {tex}{1 \over 2}\sqrt {61} {/tex}

questions.explanation: We have: {tex}​​\overrightarrow {OA} = - \hat i + {1 \over 2}\hat j + 4\hat k{/tex} (position vector of A) similarly , {tex}\overrightarrow {OB} = \hat i + {1 \over 2}\hat j + 4\hat k{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow {OC} = \hat i - {1 \over 2}\hat j + 4\hat k{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow {OD} = - \hat i - {1 \over 2}\hat j + 4\hat k{/tex} : ,where {tex}\overrightarrow {AB} = \overrightarrow {OB} - \overrightarrow {OA} = \left( {\hat i + {1 \over 2}\hat j + 4\hat k} \right) - \left( { - \hat i + {1 \over 2}\hat j + 4\hat k} \right) = 2\hat i + 0\hat j + 0\hat k{/tex} (by triangle law of vector addition),similarly {tex}\overrightarrow {AD} = 0\hat i - \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex} , Therefore , area of rectangle ABCD is given by {tex}\left| {\overrightarrow {AB} X} \right.\left. {\overrightarrow {AD} } \right|{/tex} ,where {tex}\overrightarrow {AB} X\overrightarrow {AD} = {/tex} {tex}\left| {\matrix{ {\hat i} & {\hat j} & {\hat k} \cr 2 & 0 & 0 \cr 0 & { - 1} & 0 \cr } } \right| = \hat i\left( {0 - 0} \right) - \hat j\left( {0 - 0} \right) + \hat k\left( { - 2 - 0} \right){/tex} = {tex} - 2\hat k{/tex} , {tex}\left| {\overrightarrow {AB} X} \right.\left. {\overrightarrow {AD} } \right|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt {{0^2} + {0^2} + {{\left( { - 2} \right)}^2}} = {/tex} 2 sq. units.

questions.question_html: The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2\hat i - 4\hat j + 5\hat k\;{/tex} and {tex}\hat i - 2\hat j - 3\hat k{/tex}. Find the unit vector parallel to its diagonal. Also, find its area.
questions.explanation: We have, Diagonal AC as, {tex}\vec{AC}=(\hat i - 2\hat j -3\hat k )+(2\hat i-4\hat j +5\hat k)=3\hat i-6\hat j+2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec{AC}|=\sqrt{(3)^2+(-6)^2+(2)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex} A unit vector parallel to {tex}\vec AC{/tex} is given by: {tex}\frac{\vec{AC}}{|\vec {AC}|}=\frac{3\hat i -6\hat j+2\hat k}{7}= \frac{3}{7}\hat i-\frac{6}{7}\hat j+\frac{2}{7}\hat k{/tex} Also, area of parallelogram {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec{AB}\times\vec{AD}|{/tex} {tex} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&{\hat j}&{\hat k} \\ 2&{ - 4}&5 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = {tex}|22\hat i +11 \hat j|{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{22^2+11^2}=\sqrt{605}=11\sqrt5{/tex}
